overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic and professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. With a total follower base of 3,580, his content focuses heavily on computational social science, disinformation, and research tools. His audience is highly engaged, particularly on Bluesky, which drives the highest proportional engagement, while LinkedIn serves as the key hub for high-reach professional milestones and recruiting.

contentThemes.description: Research findings, academic papers, and methodology discussions around identifying information operations and coordinated link-sharing behavior.
Recruitment posts for postdoctoral researchers, PhD opportunities, and updates regarding research team growth.
Technical updates and announcements regarding software tools developed by the research team, specifically CooRnet.

recommendations: Leverage LinkedIn's large follower base with more visual media and links to convert high potential reach into higher engagement rates.
Maintain a steady posting cadence on Bluesky, as it currently demonstrates the highest community responsiveness and active interaction.
Incorporate clear call-to-actions or open-ended questions in Mastodon posts to stimulate technical discussions and increase sharing.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most responsive platform, yielding the highest average engagement rate (6.14%). It is highly effective for sharing immediate research outcomes, paper publications, and presentation announcements.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a highly dedicated, academic-focused audience interested in methodology and open software updates. While smaller in scale, it acts as a reliable channel for technical niche discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower footprint and generates the highest absolute engagement numbers. It excels at professional milestones, such as research grant awards and job vacancies.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
